% Machine-generated by scripts/make_worked_example.py.
% Frozen run hash: 9d2658d6d147de10.

\paragraph{Design and locked constants.}
The worked example is generated from the same frozen benchmark output directory as the operating-characteristics table. For run \texttt{9d2658d6d147de10}, the representative supported injected-residual draw is replicate \(408\) with base seed \(102\), and the representative clean-null draw is replicate \(219\) with base seed \(101\).
The representative indices are read from \texttt{outputs/9d2658d6d147de10/summary/representative\_index.json}. Headline decision objects are read from the frozen raw per-replicate CSV rows, while the bin means and participant slopes are recomputed deterministically from the same representative replicate and scenario configuration.
Both examples use \(P=24\) participants and the assigned-delay grid \(\tau_L\in\{0, 5, 10, 15, 20\}\,\mathrm{ms}\), with \(\sigma_\tau=0.00707\,\mathrm{s}\) on the second scale used by the slope estimator.
In the supported draw, the label-blind residual scale is \(\sigma^{\mathrm{blind}}_{\mathrm{resid}}=1.06\,\mu\mathrm{V}\), the retained-trial yield is \(\bar n_{\mathrm{ret}}=47.6\), and the registered resolution floor is
\[
\beta_{\min}=\kappa\,\frac{\sigma^{\mathrm{blind}}_{\mathrm{resid}}}{\sigma_\tau\sqrt{\bar n_{\mathrm{ret}}}}=43.5\,\mu\mathrm{V\,s^{-1}} .
\]
This floor is a resolvability threshold for the participant-level slope, not a biological importance threshold and not a population \(p\)-value.

\paragraph{Supported injected-residual draw.}
The injected-residual generator adds a negative endpoint-level slope before the comparator is fitted. The forward-only comparator is label-blind, the cross-fitted residuals are frozen, and the same decision rule used in the full benchmark is then applied.
The retained residual bin means in \Cref{tab:si-worked-bins} show the delay-ordered residual pattern for this representative draw. The participant slopes in \Cref{tab:si-worked-slopes} are the independent units entering the equal-participant estimand. Their mean is \(\widehat\beta_\tau=-60.5\,\mu\mathrm{V\,s^{-1}}\), with participant-level standard error \(4.8\,\mu\mathrm{V\,s^{-1}}\).

The assignment-calibrated randomisation test asks whether the observed negative slope is unusually negative under the assignment mechanism, with the frozen residual array held fixed. The participant-bootstrap bound asks whether the population participant-level slope is resolved beyond the registered floor. In this representative injected-residual draw, the randomisation criterion passes, the bootstrap-\(t\) upper bound lies below \(-\beta_{\min}\), the analysable participant count meets \(N_{\min}\), the support-blocking audits do not fire, the collider diagnostic does not fire, and the selection gate passes. The executable classifier therefore returns \texttt{supported}.

\paragraph{Clean-null draw and contrast.}
The clean-null draw uses the same endpoint construction, comparator, residual-freezing rule, inference, audits, and classifier, but its generator contains no assigned-delay residual. This example is not expected to give an exactly zero slope in finite Monte Carlo data. It is expected to fail the directional support rule while retaining a valid analysis sample. The final side-by-side trace in \Cref{tab:si-worked-decision} shows that the same locked pipeline supports the injected-residual draw and returns \texttt{forward\_only\_adequate} for the clean-null draw.
